title: Gantt view export
excerpt: While Gantt view is awesome, for executive reporting time and again it needs to be exported to PowerPoint. Currently this feature is not available, neuther can we print the Gantt view in PDF.  This becomes evern more cri…

posts.author: mbhatia2 (Manish Bhatia)
posts.cookedHtml: While Gantt view is awesome, for executive reporting time and again it needs to be exported to PowerPoint. Currently this feature is not available, neuther can we print the Gantt view in PDF. This becomes evern more critical when we are maintaining roadmaps on Monday.

posts.author: ksmith_brooklynva (Kelly Smith)
posts.cookedHtml: Completely agree with this comment. The Gantt view has been excellent for our customers but if they want to show it in a presentation to executives we currently have to take a screenshot instead of being able to export into PowerPoint. This would be a great feature.

posts.author: Izobelle (Isabelle)
posts.cookedHtml: True!! the Gantt view is amazing and if I want to show into directors I need this in ppt or at leat pdf

posts.author: dralph (Daniel)
posts.cookedHtml: They have place export to PDF in Beta but currently it will on PDF a screen shot of the Gantt Screen, which if you have more than 12 tasks in a project, you lose everything past the first couple of tasks.

posts.author: jmelhorn (Josh M)
posts.cookedHtml: This really needs to be fixed, Monday! Please help @dipro ! Thanks!

posts.author: dipro (Dipro Bhowmik)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey y’all, do you have any examples of what format of powerpoints or PDFs you want to see?

Our current platform doesn’t support this, but I can add this to our App Idea Center to be picked up by a third-party builder!

posts.author: echologist (Echologist)
posts.cookedHtml: Exporting Gantt-Charts to Excel does not seem to working. It is it possible to export the Gantt-Chart as a PDF instead?

posts.author: oleputer (Elizabeth Parvarandeh)
posts.cookedHtml: We are looking to print our Gantt Chart from Monday.com as well.

posts.author: BB1 (Ben)
posts.cookedHtml: Please make printing Gantt charts easier… it would be good to be able to customise the print area

posts.author: Vickyblue21 (Fen-Yu (Vicky) Lin)
posts.cookedHtml:
Same issue here. It would be really helpful if it becomes possible to export Gantt-charts to a PDF or to an Excel in its original form (shown on Monday.com). Now the Gantt chart is shown really differently when you export it to Excel. This was not what we expected…

Is it possible to put this in the development plan of Monday?

posts.author: Joris (Joris Nieuwint)
posts.cookedHtml: Indeed, the Excel export isn’t something you can share with a client.

posts.author: BridgewaterStudio (Scott Billik)
posts.cookedHtml: would be nice as well need to share production schedules

posts.author: DebStat21 (Deborah)
posts.cookedHtml: Is there any estimate timeframe on properly printing the ganatt chart or awknowledgement that this is a required feature for users? Monday, you are great, but we need this feature!

posts.author: pkane (Patrick Kane)
posts.cookedHtml: I’m also in the same boat. Export to excel is adequate at best, but needs a significant amount of work to get into shareable format, making it basically pointless. The only other option for me is to take screen shots and piece together views, which is just not cutting it. Export to PDF is a critical feature, and I really would like the team at Monday.com to develop this.

posts.author: Liz.harding (Liz Harding)
posts.cookedHtml: I would also like to vote for this please - exporting a gantt chart (with option to show columns from task list too) is essential.

posts.author: m809 (maggie louie)
posts.cookedHtml: Can we also export a graphical view of gantt charts so that one see the dependency as the excel file does that offer that.

posts.author: MarianaAlmeida (Mariana Almeida)
posts.cookedHtml: I also need to export the Gantt as a PDF. Any updates on that feature being created?

posts.author: PaulMessink (Paul Messink)
posts.cookedHtml: Need the ability to share our schedule in Gannt view, via PDF. When is this happening?

posts.author: KMcG (Kelly McGarity)
posts.cookedHtml: This feature would be so helpful. It is now January 2023… is this something that will be implemented?

posts.author: bbferr (bruno)
posts.cookedHtml:
Gantt functionality is absolutelly useless without a proper export or print format.
existing excel export is very bad
This is a enormous flaw for this platform.

Is there any updates on this issue?

posts.author: Universe (Universe)
posts.cookedHtml: Any update on this issue? Would be very helpful if we have a complete and clean view of the gantt chart in pdf or ppt. Really needed for reports!

posts.author: Camille34 (Camille Perez)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hello Mondays,

GANTT view was one of the reason we choose Monday. But today is not complete. The view online is really small and really not easy to read for big project. Moreover when we want to cell our Project manager that they can show the Gantt to client, stakeholders, and Municipality is not possible because we can not export a usefull and complete version of the Gantt.
Thank a lot for improve this option

posts.author: LaurenY (Lauren Yammine)
posts.cookedHtml: There are some serious limitations with the Gantt export views from Monday.com - both the print PDF and Excel views. Would it be possible to add more exporting options, especially for the PDF view? For example - an option to print the entire project in PDF view, or options for the layout of the export?

posts.author: mike.bleyenheuft (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml:
The poor exporting to Excel and PDF function is becoming a real problem with my clients who are requesting that we move to MS Projects.
We cannot export to Excel without showing sub-items under headers. And the rendering of sub-items includes “undefined” next to it.

posts.author: simon.mcgoldrick (Simon McGoldrick)
posts.cookedHtml: To really provide a MS Project alternative you must be able to play with the size of the output - A0 print or longer is what we often used in MS Project to get something workable. This platform is incomplete until this is resolved…manual workers in a production environment need to see plans too and in many places it’s still done with paper

posts.author: Oneill (Sean)
posts.cookedHtml: Can’t emphasize enough how important this is. Trying to win colleauges over to Monday who are used to Project. You win on user friendliness, but dependencies (which should be STANDARD by the way) and flexible, printable GANTT views are essential.

posts.author: echan (Emily Chan)
posts.cookedHtml: Agreed. All our clients need PDF. As much as I’ve been trying to encourage adoption of viewing data live via an account, some users just are not interested. Especially at the executive level.

posts.author: LipinsM (Mike)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a simple request. Please improve exporting options. What is the status of this, Monday?

posts.author: ECO_Blair (Blair Cole)
posts.cookedHtml: The ability to export a Gantt that shows dependencies is a MUST

posts.author: RSpear (Randy Spear)
posts.cookedHtml:
Need the ability to export to other gantt formats (MS project).
In order to be able to work efficiently with external partners.

posts.author: RSpear (Randy Spear)
posts.cookedHtml:
Currently you can only export an 8.5x11 pdf and it defaults back to month view resulting in a condensed version of what you’d like
Most project managers need to be able to see a full legible timeline.

Requesting the ability to print a desired timeline (days/weeks/months/etc) view with page setup options.

posts.author: MaxWoodyWorld (Max)
posts.cookedHtml:
This feature is highly requested already by a lot of user since the export to Excel doesn’t work at all and it takes away all dependencies for instance.

@monday Please make an export to pdf with and end and start date possible…

THANKS!!!

posts.author: Mchartrand (Michele Chartrand)
posts.cookedHtml:
I agree, we tried to present something to our customers today and it doesn’t look professional at all with excel!

Is this something in the pipeline ? Do you have an ETA ? Thanks

posts.author: LaurenY (Lauren Yammine)
posts.cookedHtml: There are some serious limitations with the Gantt export views from Monday.com - both the print PDF and Excel views. Would it be possible to add more exporting options, especially for the PDF view? For example - an option to print the entire project in PDF view, or options for the layout of the export?

posts.author: CharlieFacade (Charlie Kickham)
posts.cookedHtml:
I cannot comprehend how this platform doesn’t do this very basic and very essential feature… sigh.

Not only should it already have the feature from day dot, but they should’ve updated it immediately after seeing this feedback over a year ago. What are they doing???!!! What good is a Ferrari with no wheels?!
Unbelievable!!

posts.author: Randall (Randall )
posts.cookedHtml: This is a pain point for our company to move forward with utilizing Monday. It would be nice to export as a pdf or image so we can print or display the entire gantt in one view. The excel view is complete garbage. Is there any update to getting this feature?

posts.author: tbaustert (Todd Baustert)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a pain point for our agency as well. We really need the ability to export as a PDF or image and display the whole Gantt chart. It would also be helpful to be able to increase the font sizes of the items prior to the export. We meet frequently with parties where displaying the online gantt isn’t possible or appropriate. This has the potential to limit adoption of Monday.com within our organization - which is a shame as the platform has potential to address many of our use cases.

posts.author: echan (Emily Chan)
posts.cookedHtml: This is a huge pain point for our small consulting business. All clients request reports in PDF. It’s the current convention. Not having it impacts our relationship when doing executive reporting.

posts.author: Vilstrup (Marcus)
posts.cookedHtml:
So this post has been written in 2021, almost 3 years ago and there still hasn’t been found a fix? Compaired to all the different features Monday.com is capable of showing, how is an export with dependencies not a thing yet?

Have anyone head back from the development team at all?

posts.author: jameswright (james)
posts.cookedHtml: If I new it would take 3 years to fix a simple printing of a Gantt chart there is no way I would have recommended this product to our organization. Really is terrible.

posts.author: tylerrake (K)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi
We want to print /save to pdf (or jpeg) the whole complete Gantt chart especially big Gantt chart that span more than 1 A4 page.
Problem with “Print Board” feature for Gantt View is it can print/save to pdf but not whole Gantt chart- it can only print/save to pdf whatever you see on first page and not the next pages in one pdf file.
We reduced the “scale” in “Print Board” and also changed page to A3 size to fit whole Gantt chart into 1 page but bottom part of Gantt chart is missing. We tried a Chrome extension called GoFullPage but it only captures first page and not all pages. Does anyone know of workaround solution either using third part apps or add on or extension ?
Would not mind paying some reasonable fee to a consultant/specialist who can provide a workaround solution that will fix/solve this problem.

Thanks in advance

posts.author: Shreeya (Shreeya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi,
The Gantt pdfs really need some improvement
Firstly, it does not show any dependencies.
Secondly, the printed view does capture all the items properly
This is a very important feature as printed views are important to easily share data. Currently, I need to take multiple screenshots of a schedule which runs over more than 5 pages which is costing me a lot of time.
I request @dipro @mbhatia2 to really look into this issue

posts.author: GanttGo (Hidai Bar-Mor)
posts.cookedHtml: For anyone looking for more export options - we built GanttGo for this. It exports multipage PDFs with dependency arrows included, plus Excel, PowerPoint and PNG. Also supports holidays/non-working days, critical path, and subitems. Available in the marketplace - search “Ganttgo”.

posts.author: Iryna_Belaya (Iryna Belaya)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi to everyone in this post,

I wanted to mention another option for those who collaborate on timelines with clients, contractors, or other third parties outside of monday.com.

Export Gantt → attach PDF → send email → wait for feedback → update timeline → export again → resend.

Repeat. And repeat. And repeat.

Somewhere along the way:

• no one remembers which version is the latest
• timeline changes get buried in long emails
• approvals take days
• testing gets delayed
• last-minute pressure before launch becomes inevitable

We developed the Interactive Gantt Timeline app, that allows to share Gantt with clients and collaborate directly on it:

  • Clients can leave comments and suggest timeline changes right on Gantt

    leave suggestions2045×1045 312 KB

  • PMs get notification about comments and suggestions, and can test different scenarios, and then approve the appropriate timeline.

    test versions gantt1581×993 212 KB

After approving, new timeline automatically updates the board.

Interactive Gantt Timeline doesn’t replace monday.com’s native Gantt — it enhances it.
Keep using monday.com’s Gantt view internally, and share Interactive Gantt Timeline with clients outside your workspace.

No version chaos.
No endless PDF exports.
Just one live Gantt for monday.com.

You can install and test the app on your account.

posts.author: MickaylaM (Mickayla Murray)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hey everyone, I know this one has been a long time coming. We hear you loud and clear on how much you need to export and print your Gantt views to keep things running smoothly.

I’m happy to share that this feature is officially in the works! I can’t give you a hard date just yet, but the team is actively building. I’ll be sure to update you here the moment I have more to share. Thanks for sticking with us while our product team works to bring this to life!
